Directionally drilled under river duct route containing 3x125mm SDPE ducts which are designed to contain telecoms copper and/or fibre optic cable as well as copper signalling and power cabling if required. From here it extends 38m to the edge of the river at a depth of 7.2m, as per the diagram below. New type of level crossing warning system installed which provides warnings for users . Red lights and Yodalarms are provided together with rail mounted sensors at the crossing . Equipment cases are provided and the system is powered . No electrical hazard exists from sensors, connecting  cabling , lights or yodalarm systems but risk does exist for the power cables supplying the system .The system is remotely monitored and automatically reports system failures and component damage to route control .  Staff should NOT rely on the system to warn of trains . Staff should not touch any of the equipment unless they are authorised and competent to do so . Any damage caused to the system or any of its components should be reported immediately to Control .

Local Name: Thorpe Le Soken Station Car Park & Access Track
Free Text:
The pipe is 220mm diameter Cast Iron and sits approximately 0.75m below ground level. It is suspected to be redundant however this has not been confirmed by any testing. The function and source of the pipe is not known. Various attempts were made to trace the pipe however they were not successful. It is suspected to be foul drainage related to a nearby malting house or previous redundant sidings however these assumptions were not confirmed. A drawing markup is attached showing its approximate location. The pipe was uncovered beneath the maintenance access track approx 3.4m from the redundant platform wall, it extends towards the London end and bends beneath the platform approx 40m to the wast. The pipe was traced and is suspected to run east through the car park past the station building, it is then suspected to connect to the south to the vegetated area.           ~
